title: Module Registry
description: The canonical, static list of Module Registry descriptors — Phase 2B Batch PXP-3 (docs/44-PHASE-2B-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §7.1/§17/§22, ADR-012 (amended), docs/47-PHASE-2B-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md). Defines which patient-facing capabilities exist on the platform at all (availability) — per-patient enablement is a separate, Sheet-backed concern (shared/schemas/patient-module-state.schema.json). Seeded, in Batch PXP-3, with only the three already-implemented Phase 2A capabilities — a new module belonging to a not-yet-built capability is added as its own registry entry by the batch that actually designs and builds it (docs/47 §4), never pre-declared by an earlier batch guessing its data_source/shape. Batch PXP-5 adds a fourth entry ('daily_checkin', docs/44 §10/§11/§22) and Batch PXP-7 adds a fifth ('care_plan', docs/44 §12/§22) — exactly this growth pattern; Calculators remain deliberately not pre-declared for their own future batch. **Batch PXP-10 (docs/44 §10.1/§22, docs/47) removes the 'symptom_tracker' entry** — Daily Check-in (PXP-5) proven in production is this migration's replacement; SymptomLogs rows are retained permanently and the standalone Symptom History page remains reachable, only the dashboard entry point and Module Registry availability are retired (see module-registry.md's own 'Batch PXP-10 removal' section). **Batch WPI-11 (docs/56-WPI-11-HOLOSCAN-ARCHITECTURE-FREEZE.md §18.1, ADR-024/025/026) adds a sixth entry, 'holoscan'** — the patient-facing Holoscan photo-capture card, backed by a new, real, authenticated data_source route (get_holoscan_recognitions); fail-closed by PatientModuleState absence, the same default every existing entry already has (ADR-010) — no new ADR was required for this half, since ADR-010's existing guarantee already fully covers it. See module-registry.md for the field-by-field explanation, which fields are reserved/inert extension points, and which fields this batch actually consumes. apps-script/ModuleRegistry.gs hand-ports this list into its own Foundation-prefixed array, the same convention condition-slugs.json's consumers already use.

modules.title: Timeline
modules.description: A merged, reverse-chronological feed of consultation history and other health events.

modules.title: Reports
modules.description: Uploaded lab results, prescriptions, and prior medical records.

modules.title: Daily Check-in
modules.description: A short, doctor-assigned daily check-in on how you are feeling today.

modules.title: Care Plan
modules.description: Your doctor-authored goals, instructions, and next review date.

modules.title: Medication Photo Scan
modules.description: Photograph a medicine you are currently taking so your doctor can review and confirm it as part of your medication history.
